center.name: 旧大陆
center.summary: 旧大陆是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括大红鹳、Black-necked stilt、领燕鸻等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: epirhinus}} | synonyms = * Buceros nasutus * Tockus nasutus }} The African grey hornbill (Lophoceros nasutus) is a member of the hornbill family of mainly tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its genus is presently monotypic and seems to be closely related to the similar genus Allopyge, found in Old World cranes.Dronen et al

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Congo pied hornbill (Lophoceros fasciatus) is a bird of the hornbill family, a family of tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World. The Congo pied hornbill is native to the Congo Basin

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Old World tropics from west Africa to Japan and Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Hornbills are a family of tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: semifasciatus, Ghana | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Lophoceros | species = semifasciatus | authority = (Hartlaub, 1855) | range_map = | range_map_caption = | synonyms = }} The West African pied hornbill (Lophoceros semifasciatus) is a bird of the hornbill family, a family of tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World. The West African pied hornbill is found in West Africa, from south Nigeria to Senegal and Gambia—primarily in secondary forest areas of the Guinean-Congolese forest, and is threatened by forest fragmentation. It was previously considered conspecific with the Congo pied hornbill, and was split in IOC 13.2. It is a frugivore.{{Cite journal |last1=Chaves |first1=Patrícia P

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is the only penguin found in the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Malabar pied hornbill (Anthracoceros coronatus), also known as the lesser pied hornbill, is a bird in the hornbill family, a family of tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Common in the Old World, they are found in Northern (coastal) and Sub-Saharan Africa, the Indian subcontinent (south of the Himalayas), the Middle East, the Levant, the Persian Gulf, the Gulf of Aden, the Red Sea, and the Mediterranean countries of Southern Europe

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The little bittern is native to the Old World, breeding in Africa, central and southern Europe, western and southern Asia, and Madagascar

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: gallinago }} The common snipe (Gallinago gallinago, formerly also known as Gallinago cælestis) is a small, stocky wader native to the Old World, where it breeds in marshes, bogs, tundra and wet meadows

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: carbo | synonyms = Pelecanus carbo }} thumb|Group of great cormorants in Latvia The great cormorant (Phalacrocorax carbo), also known as just cormorant in Britain, as black shag or kawau in New Zealand, formerly also known as the great black cormorant across the Northern Hemisphere, the black cormorant in Australia, and the large cormorant in India, is a widespread member of the cormorant family of seabirds. It breeds in much of the Old World, Australasia, and the Atlantic coast of North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The striated heron was formerly considered to be conspecific with the little heron that is found in the Old World tropics from west Africa to Japan and Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: pallida }} The eastern olivaceous warbler (Iduna pallida), known simply as the olivaceous warbler when its western relative is referred to as the 'isabelline warbler', is a small passerine bird with drab plumage tones, that is native to the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: dubius | synonyms = Charadrius dubius }} The little ringed plover (Thinornis dubius) is a small plover native to the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Deciduous woodland is a typical breeding and hunting habitat for the Eurasian sparrowhawk. A widespread species throughout the temperate and subtropical parts of the Old World, the Eurasian sparrowhawk is resident or breeds in an estimated global range of and had an estimated population of 1.5 million birds in 2009

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: As with other pratincoles, it is native to the Old World

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=421 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14481096 }} | range_map = Troglodytes troglodytes-map.jpg | range_map_caption = }} The Eurasian wren (Troglodytes troglodytes) is a very small insectivorous bird and the only member of the wren family found in the Old World, specifically in Eurasia and Africa (Maghreb)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is distributed across many parts of the Old World, across Africa, Europe, and Asia. It lives around well-vegetated marshes, ponds, canals and other wetlands. The species is not found in the polar regions or many tropical rainforests; generally it is one of the most common Old World rail species, together with the Eurasian coot in some regions
